original_abstract: The current progress in the improvement of membrane materials enlarges the utilizations and necessities for innovative, more efficient, resistant, and highly specialized separation materials. One of the growing areas of the implementation of membrane-based strategies is the selective separation of rare earth elements (REEs). Owing to their high potential and essential meaning in modern industry, demands for raw REEs have increased significantly in the last years. Moreover, considering the fact that isolation of REEs has listened as one of “the seven chemical separation processes that, if improved, would rear great global benefits” their application is emerging. Membrane strategies are gaining popularity in metal separation and wastewater treatment as sustainable green approaches with a simple operation. Many membrane strategies for REEs separation have been developed, but only a few reviews have been found. The main message coming from the literature review is that more light should be shed on the progress and implementation of non-liquid membranes. The previously utilized liquid membranes faced many problems related mainly to the long-term utilization, stability, and extraction yield. As a consequence, the REEs separation processes have moved in the direction of much more durable materials, i.e., non-liquid membrane strategies, in which the
humanized_abstract: Progresses in the development of membrane materials open new possibilities for innovative, more efficient, robust and specialized membrane materials. Nowadays, membrane technologies have a large number of possible applications, especially in the emerging field of separation of rare earth elements (REEs). As many studies demonstrate, REEs are indispensable elements for various applications and their demand has increased dramatically in recent years, because isolation of REEs is one of “the seven chemical separation processes that, if improved, would reap great global benefits”. Furthermore, membrane approaches for the separation of metals and wastewater treatment are widely accepted as sustainable “green” alternatives with simple procedures. Several membrane approaches have been developed for the recovery and separation of REEs. Even though there have been only a few reviews dealing with membrane processes in general for REEs separation, one of the major conclusions obtained from the literature review is that there should be more information available concerning non-liquid membranes compared to liquid membranes. Liquid membranes have been developed based on several concepts such as supported liquid membrane, Hollow fiber permeator and others, but the number of researches dealing with liquid membranes faced several problems during their long-term stability, membrane extraction yield, as well as leakage. Consequently, many processes have focused on utilizing more durable materials in REEs separation, pointing the direction towards non-liquid membrane strategies, in which the liquid phase is replaced by a solid inorganic or organic material.
